label: Other ways to access a service
description: Provide alternative channels for users to access and complete a service when the primary route is not suitable. All channels must lead to the same service outcome.
use_when: Not all users can access the primary (often digital) channel.
Services must support assisted, offline, or in-person access.
Policy or inclusion requirements mandate alternative routes.
do_not_use_when: All users can access the primary channel and no inclusion requirements apply.

building_blocks.notes: For sending reference numbers or instructions to the user.

steps.label: Make alternative routes visible
steps.description: Alternative routes are presented on the service sheet: phone, in-person, assisted, paper, delegated access.

steps.label: Explain how alternative access works
steps.description: Tell users how to start the alternative route, what information is needed, whether progress carries across channels, and any differences in timing or cost.

steps.label: Handover into alternative channel
steps.description: Support a clean transition: book an appointment, generate a reference number, create an assisted case, or issue instructions.

ux_considerations.text: Alternatives must be discoverable early, not only after digital failure.
Present alternatives as legitimate options, not secondary or last resort.
Alternative routes must not reduce eligibility or service quality.
Staff using assisted routes must follow the same service rules and validations.

technical_notes.text: Users must not need to repeat information when switching channels. Progress must carry across.
